item.title: readme
item.description: web / gif Category - HARD Description - I made a secure php web app where I can upload all my gifs.

item.title: readme
item.description: The Server From Hell Tryhackme The Server From Hell - Linux - Medium tryhackme.com/room/theserverfromhell Nmap # Nmap 7.93 scan initiated Sun Jun 11 15:46:45 2023 as: nmap -sC -sV -oA nmap/theserverfromhell 10.10.160.231 Nmap scan report for 10.10.160.231 Host is up (0.20s latency).

item.description: Startup Tryhackme Startup - Linux - Easy tryhackme.com/room/startup Nmap └──╼ $nmap -sC -sV -oA nmap/startup 10.10.163.144 Starting Nmap 7.93 ( nmap.org ) at 2023-06-04 00:20 IST Nmap scan report for 10.10.163.144 Host is up (0.15s latency).

item.title: readme
item.description: Revenge Revenge - Linux - Medium tryhackme.com/room/revenge Revenge - Linux - Medium After checkign website, I thought of using sql injection in /product/:id parameter └──╼ $sqlmap -u 10.10.133.191/products/1 --dump ___ __H__ ___ ___["]_____ ___ ___ {1.6.12#stable} |_ -| .

item.title: day2
item.description: [Day 2] [Web Exploitation] The Elf Strikes Back! Description This was a lot of information, so let’s put it all together and look at the full process for exploiting a file upload vulnerability in a PHP web application: Find a file upload point.
